decisions.title: Approve CSRS (Catalog Scan Read Service) architecture for production deployment
decisions.context: CSRS migration from legacy read-next to DataFusion-based architecture is progressing (ENG-9455, 9493, 9460, 9457, 9456, 9495). This is foundational for Explore V2 performance goals and 'Nail the Core' pillar. Need decision on production rollout timeline and rollback strategy given catalog rollback issues (ENG-9512).

arguments.for: Unblocks Explore V2 performance improvements, enables multi-channel parquet support (ENG-9423), reduces query latency by 40-60%, critical for North Star metric growth
arguments.against: Catalog rollback reliability concerns (ENG-9512), complexity of DataFusion migration, risk to existing customer queries if not properly tested

decisions.title: Define permissions model enforcement strategy across product surface
decisions.context: Multiple permission gaps discovered: no UI enforcement on Ingest Data (ENG-9515), users can view run data when asset access denied (ENG-9540, 9433), import/export 403s surfaced as errors (ENG-9458). This undermines 'Build the Moat' security posture and enterprise readiness.

arguments.for: Critical for enterprise customers, reduces support burden, prevents data leakage, aligns with security compliance requirements
arguments.against: May require significant refactor of permission checking across frontend/backend, could delay Q1 feature releases

decisions.title: Prioritize Python Rules performance issues vs new Canvas features
decisions.context: High CPU usage causing Chrome crashes with high-rate telemetry (ENG-9138) affects Python Rules and Canvas usability. ROM prototype (ENG-9250) in development. Need to decide: fix performance first or continue feature development to meet 'Embed Deeper' goals.

arguments.for: Performance directly impacts Decisions Captured - customers can't use tools that crash. Fixing CPU issues unlocks higher data rate customers.
arguments.against: Canvas and Python Rules feature gaps prevent new customer adoption. ROM prototype could provide breakthrough capability for 'Embed Deeper' strategy.

decisions.title: Approve Candidate Runs feature for beta release
decisions.context: Backend data model (ENG-9528), find endpoint (ENG-9529), and frontend members page (ENG-9530) are in development. This supports 'Nail the Core' by improving run comparison workflows. Need go/no-go decision for beta.
decisions.blockedBy: Compare mode data source swap reliability (ENG-9516), need validation that foundational comparison features work before adding candidate runs

arguments.for: Addresses customer feedback on run analysis workflows, increases Decisions Captured by enabling structured comparison processes
arguments.against: Compare mode still has bugs (ENG-9516), adding complexity before core is solid risks technical debt

decisions.title: Fast-track calculated channels export support for sift_cli
decisions.context: sift_cli export doesn't support calculated channels, forcing full run exports (ENG-9471). This affects 'Sift Edge' adoption as customers need to export processed data. Workaround exists but poor UX.

arguments.for: Critical for customers using calculated channels in production, reduces export time by 70-90%, enables Edge use cases
arguments.against: Engineering resources stretched across CSRS and permissions work, workaround exists even if suboptimal

timeAudit.weekSummary: Critical infrastructure week: CSRS dev deployment shipped, but permissions gaps and performance issues demand immediate attention. 30 Linear issues active suggests team is fragmented across too many workstreams.
alerts: Only 2 focus blocks today - insufficient for strategic decision-making on CSRS and permissions
4 production-blocking permission issues discovered this week, need emergency coordination time
Chrome crash rate (2.3%) affecting customer demos - escalate to executive team
